title: Dichanthelium xanthophysum
taxonomy: Dichanthelium xanthophysum is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Panicoideae. Within this subfamily, it is placed in the tribe Paniceae. This classification is further supported by its inclusion in the genus Dichanthelium. As a member of the Poaceae family, it shares characteristics with other grasses.

intro: Dichanthelium xanthophysum, also known as yellow panicgrass, is a species of grass native to North America, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Dichanthelium xanthophysum include sesquiterpenes, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dichanthelium xanthophysum is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as salves and poultices, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
